betterquesting:10.desc:8:
One of the greatest aspects of nature is its diversity. No uniqueness. The uniqueness of those that bear witness to reality, and make what they witness their own. 
I can safely say that each individual is truly unique, and that one's self never stops trying to affirm itself, even when existence as an individual is challenged. Creeponia, Barathos, Iromine; people created to serve; people in slavery; people that, no matter their situation, affirmed themselves as people, and not instruments.
That too, is worthiness.
Saying "I am" when the world around you claims to determine who you are. An act so simple and yet so vital.
And one I am happy to see recognized here in this place.
